title.narrative: Wasichana Wetu Wafaulu - Let Our Girls Succeed

description.narrative: The Wasichana Wetu Wafaulu (WWW - Let Our Girls Succeed) Project is a follow-on project to Wasichana Wote Wasome project implemented 2013-2017. This WWW project (2017-2023) is funded by DFID under the Girls’ Education Challenge window works in 521 Primary, 45 Secondary schools, 25 TVETs and the communities they serve in eight Counties namely: Nairobi and Mombasa (urban slums) Kilifi, Kwale, Tana-River, Turkana, Marsabit and Samburu (Arid and Semi-Arid Lands). The project using an integrated approach will employ strategies that address the girl herself, the girl at home, the girl at school and the girl in the community to support existing cohort of girls (currently in classes 5 – 8) to complete their current phase of education, achieve improved learning outcomes and transition successfully to a productive and positive next phase.
Ananda Marga Universal Relief Team Q20 Disbursement
Primary and Secondary School Teacher Training
Cluster Meetings - Primary and Secondary Schools
Teacher Coaching and Mentorship for Primary
Provision of Teaching Aids for Secondary Schools
Organising and Running of Community Theme Days
Secondary School Patrons Peer Educators meetings
Joint Monitoring Visits Consortium Lead and Partners
M&E Staff Time - EDT and Partners
Ananda Marga Universal Relief Team Q18 Disbursement
Ananda Marga Universal Relief Team Q18 Disbursement
Provision of roving tutors to support learning; including SNE teachers
Teacher training - primary and secondary schools
Teacher coaching and mentorship - primary schools
Support for continued teaching by BoM & remedial teachers
Enhanced and effective CoPs for school leaders (h/ teachers; deputy h/teachers and directors)
Enhanced and effective CoPs for primary and secondary teachers
Support for staff communication and stationery
Tutor support for catch up and remedial teaching
